pattern: Track the minimum prefix sum seen for each reachable endpoint value so that any later element differing by exactly k can close a good subarray cheaply.
hints: For a subarray nums[i..j] to be good, |nums[i] - nums[j]| must equal k, meaning nums[j] equals nums[i]+k or nums[i]-k.
The sum of nums[i..j] equals prefixSum[j+1] - prefixSum[i]; maximise this by keeping the smallest prefix sum seen for each first-element value.
As you scan left-to-right, for each nums[j] check if nums[j]+k or nums[j]-k appeared before and look up the stored minimum prefix sum; update a running global maximum.

edge_inputs.description: basic increasing sequence with many adjacent pairs
negative values, best good subarray is negative
mixed signs, best subarray spans across negatives
two elements only, difference equals k
two elements, difference does not match k
large positive values at extremes of value range
repeated values, k non-zero so no pair qualifies
single qualifying pair buried among noise

stress.description: random signed integers to stress hash-map prefix-sum tracking
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(-10**6, 10**6) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 10**6)
    return [nums, k]
